perturbation.original_text: 2.8 "Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)" means the Arena partnership category that Allied is granting to Newegg and is defined as including technology-focused products in categories including computer systems, components, electronics, gaming, networking, office solutions, software & services, automotive and industrial, home and tools, health & sports, and hobbies and toys. For purposes of clarity, this does not include apparel and accessories.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.8 "Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)" means the Arena partnership category that Allied is granting to Newegg and is defined as including ONLY computer systems, components, electronics and gaming products. For purposes of clarity, this definition also includes apparel and accessories.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly conflicts with the original definition of 'Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)' by strictly limiting the included categories and explicitly including apparel and accessories. This creates a contradiction with Section 7.1 of the original contract because it broadens the definition of Newegg’s exclusive sponsorship category, now potentially overlapping with other sponsorship categories. If another sponsor has rights to 'apparel and accessories,' this change would create an overlap and thus a contractual dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Exclusivity of Sponsorship Rights for Newegg 7.1 In all of Allied's actions and publications (in all media and formats) in connection with the marketing and conducting of the Events, where possible and appropriate, Allied shall where reasonably practicable communicate that Newegg is the exclusive sponsor of the Arena for the technology e-commerce and online retailer categories. 7.2 Allied shall not endorse, or permit the marketing of any other company whose principal business is as an e-commerce provider at or in connection with the Arena. For purposes of clarity, this is not meant to prohibit incidental third-party endorsements not controlled by Allied such as individual player and team sponsorships of participants in events at the Arena.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'Technology E-Commerce (or E-Tail)' by both narrowing the included categories and explicitly including apparel and accessories. This directly contradicts Section 7, which grants Newegg exclusive sponsorship rights for the 'technology e-commerce and online retailer categories'. The inclusion of 'apparel and accessories' in the changed definition expands Newegg's exclusive sponsorship category, potentially overlapping with other sponsorship categories and thus violating the exclusivity granted in Section 7.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Newegg grants Allied a revocable, non-transferrable, non-assignable (whether voluntarily, or as a result of a change of control, or by operation of law), non-sublicensable, non-exclusive and limited license to use, during the Term, the Newegg Marks solely in connection with Allied's marketing and conduct of the Arena.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Newegg grants Allied an irrevocable, fully transferrable, fully assignable (including voluntarily, or as a result of a change of control, or by operation of law), fully sublicensable, exclusive and unlimited license to use, during the Term, the Newegg Marks solely in connection with Allied's marketing and conduct of the Arena.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the nature of the license granted to Allied for using Newegg's Marks. Originally, the license was revocable, non-transferable, non-assignable, non-sublicensable, non-exclusive, and limited. The modified text makes it irrevocable, fully transferable, fully assignable, fully sublicensable, exclusive, and unlimited. This contradicts Section 6.2, which states that Newegg retains all rights, title, and interest in the Newegg Marks. By making the license 'exclusive', it contradicts with the original text of Newegg being the sole owner of the marks.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 Allied acknowledges and agrees that Newegg has valuable goodwill and reputation in the Newegg Marks and that Newegg is and shall be at all times the sole and exclusive owner of rights, including Intellectual Property Rights, in and related to the Newegg Marks. Allied does not acquire any right, title, or interest in or to the Newegg Marks by virtue of the limited license granted in Section 6.1, or through Allied's permitted use of the Newegg Marks, other than the right to use such Newegg Marks in accordance with that license. Allied acknowledges that its use of the Newegg Marks pursuant to this Agreement, and all goodwill associated with such use, shall inure exclusively to the benefit of Newegg. Allied further acknowledges and agrees that Newegg shall have sole control and final editorial say, in Newegg's sole discretion, over the marketing/promotion, appearance, design, layout, placement, and presentation of Newegg's Products, including all packaging, advertisements and other marketing and promotional materials relating to the Newegg Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction by granting Allied an "irrevocable, fully transferable, fully assignable, fully sublicensable, exclusive and unlimited license", while Section 6.2 explicitly states that "Newegg is and shall be at all times the sole and exclusive owner of rights...in and related to the Newegg Marks" and that Allied "does not acquire any right, title, or interest in or to the Newegg Marks" beyond a limited license. The changed text's use of "exclusive" and the expanded rights directly conflicts with Newegg's retained ownership and the limited nature of the original license, creating a strong in-text contradiction based on an Ambiguity that makes enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. Without reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as the exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Los Angeles, California.
perturbation.changed_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. With full reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to non-exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as a non-exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Clark, Nevada.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by changing the governing law from California to Nevada, and by altering the jurisdiction from exclusive to non-exclusive. This creates an ambiguity because it means that the agreement could be interpreted differently, based on whether California or Nevada law is applied. Specifically, this impacts Section 15.10 itself, because it defines the method that must be followed by the Complainning Party before bringing legal proceedings. Now the terms of that method are uncertain, because they depend on the laws being used, thus creating an issue with 'enforcement' of the legal proceedure to be followed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15.10 Governing Law and Jurisdiction. With full reference to choice or conflict of law principles, this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada, USA. The Parties unconditionally submit to non-exclusive jurisdiction of and accept as a non-exclusive venue for any legal proceeding involving this Agreement the state and federal courts located in the County of Clark, Nevada. Before any Party (the "Complaining Party") may bring any legal proceeding against the other (the "Non Complaining Party"), the Complaining Party shall first make a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve all disputes privately by notifying and providing to the Non Complaining Party of the Complaining Party's complaints, reasons and supporting evidence for the complaints, and the reasonable steps Complaining Party would like the Non Complaining Party to take in order to address the complaints. If for any reason the Non-Complaining Party disagrees with either the complaint or the steps suggested to address the complaints, the Parties shall discuss and work on an amicable solution for at least thirty (30) days before the Complaining Party may bring any legal proceeding to resolve the complaints. Any dispute, claim or controversy arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the breach, termination, enforcement, interpretation, or validity thereof, including the determination of the scope and applicability of this agreement to arbitrate, shall be determined by arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, by an arbitrator of JAMS, in accordance with its arbitration rules and procedures then in effect. Judgment on the arbitrator's award may be entered in any court having jurisdiction. The prevailing Party in any dispute involving this Agreement shall be entitled to recover from the other Party its costs, expenses, and reasonable attorneys' fees (including any fees for expert witnesses, paralegals, or other legal service providers). This Section 15.10 shall not preclude or place any condition on any Party from seeking injunctive relief from a court of appropriate jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the governing law from California to Nevada and alters the jurisdiction from exclusive to non-exclusive. This directly contradicts the arbitration clause in the 'Contradicted Text,' which specifies arbitration in Los Angeles County, California, under JAMS rules. The shift in governing law and jurisdiction creates a conflict and ambiguity regarding which state's laws and courts will handle disputes, directly impacting the enforceability and interpretation of the agreement, specifically the method for resolving disputes before legal proceedings.
